pages.full_text:
[photo: painted + embroidered gloves, caption reads:] Musée de Millan
painted + embroidered
gloves
made in
Millan [illegible]

Monday July 31st.
By 9.45 we were at the canoe depot. They gave us wour coats + paddles + we set off, Simon + Eve in a double canoe + Charles in a single. Old houses lie on the hillside either side of the [?hot]. We disturbed 2 Heron. It was beautiful +
photo_captions: Musée de Millan painted + embroidered gloves made in Millan
pages.notes: Photo shows long gloves on blue background, likely a museum postcard or printed card. 'Millan' likely refers to Millau (Aveyron, France). Handwritten page number not visible in top-left corner. The word after 'hillside either side of the' is unclear — could be 'lot' (River Lot) or 'hot' or another word; 'lot' would make geographical sense given Entraygues context on the right page. Bottom of text continues onto right page. The number after 'Millan' in the caption may be a catalogue number. July 31st was a Monday in 2006 — confidence moderate as year is inferred from day-of-week match within volume B range.

pages.full_text:
smelt good. Simon + Eve couldn't always agree on how to paddle + steer. We reached the pick-up pace by 12 + were driven back to camp in a diesel master. With extra pull-down seats in the aisles, it could fit 20 It was very loud when it was idling, but quiet + strong going along, pulling a trailer with lots of canoes.

	In the afternoon we napped + swam + then walked into town again for suppies. The meal was delicious sausage + Aligot.

__Tuesday__. It rained before + after breakfast. We went in to see the Entraygues market + bought food + stamps.

	After lunch Eve napped + swam. Charles + Simon set off to look at the river. Simon found an old plastic downpipe. Charles dammed a section of water better. Then they came back to the van + put a stick through a plastic milk bottle + gave it 4 fins from a plastic prosciutto jacket + hung it on 2 forked sticks to spin like a mill in the waterpower. Eve found the rest of the milk in a container in the fridge, for tea
pages.notes: Page number 44 visible at top right. 'Entraygues' likely refers to Entraygues-sur-Truyère, Aveyron, France. 'Aligot' is a traditional Aveyron dish. 'suppies' likely means 'supper' or 'supplies'. 'diesel master' likely refers to a Renault Master diesel minibus used by the canoe hire company. There is a small hand-drawn sketch/doodle of a watermill device (the improvised milk-bottle mill) in the margin next to that passage — it shows a stick figure of the mill contraption with fins, but this is an inline illustration of the object described, not a route map. The text is cut off at the bottom (continues to next page). Tuesday would be 1 August if Monday was 31 July.
